Patch Set 2:

John: In changing the opc to an enum, I was following the description in the bug.  I'd be more comfortable with it as a _u64.  I don't believe the change will break compatibilty or endianness, I did as much checking on it as I could, and everything seemed functional (though I don't believe any swapping was happening between the client and server, as they were on the same architecture)

Patch Set 2:

Andreas: I thought I fixed the wire-compatability in patch 3, looks like something didn't get submitted correctly.  I run all my tests on different machines with a "pure" and a "patch" image.

Hopefully PS4 will resolve all the issues.

Patch Set 3:

Note my comment about compatibilty was on patch #2, and this problem is indeed fixed in patch #3.

I was just pointing out that your changes to wiretest.c show that you are changing the size of that structure, and if you reviewed the changes to that file (anything that results in a change to lustre_idl you could have seen this change.

parents.subject: LU-6556 obdclass: re-allow catalog to wrap around

commit.subject: LU-6746 ptlrpc: Move IT_* definitions to lustre_idl.h
commit.message:
LU-6746 ptlrpc: Move IT_* definitions to lustre_idl.h

Put IT_* definitions into an enum, as they're sent over the wire,
adjust calls, print statements, etc. to use the new enum.

Signed-off-by: Ben Evans <bevans@cray.com>
Change-Id: Ie6ad700ac185459ace72ea67563864e43c548ec3
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16228
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
